OPEN SOURCE DEMONSTRATOR

Parasoft License Monitor

Interactive dashboards and analytics for Parasoft DTP license server usage.
Built by ESL — Engineering Software Lab.

See Dashboard View on GitHub

The Problem

IT teams managing Parasoft DTP have no built-in way to track license usage trends.

🔍

No Visibility

How many licenses are in use? Which products? What are the peak times?

📄

Raw XML Logs

DTP logs are thousands of lines of raw XML — impossible to analyze manually.

💻

Multi-Platform

Usage spans Windows and Linux — hard to see the full picture.

💰

License Cost

Without usage data, teams cannot optimize seats or justify renewals.

The Solution

Automatic log parsing, interactive dashboards, CSV exports — zero external dependencies.

📊

Interactive Charts

📅

Daily / Weekly / Monthly / Yearly

📥

CSV Export

🔍

Date Filtering

🖥

Platform Breakdown

👤

Per-User Analytics

🔒

Token Auth

🌐

Multi-Server

📦

Zero Dependencies

🛡

MIT Licensed

Dashboard Preview

Real screenshots from the ESL License Monitor dashboard.

How It Works

Deploy the agent on DTP servers, run the monitor from the IT admin PC.

💻

IT Admin PC

ESL License Monitor
monitor.exe

➡
🏗

DTP Server

ESL Agent
agent.exe
Port 9271 + Token

➡
🗃

License Logs

ls_access.log*
Auto-parsed XML

Also supports local/UNC paths and multi-server aggregation via servers.json

Quick Start

Clone, point, run. Three commands to your first dashboard.

# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/zuwasi/Parasoft-License-Monitor-by-ESL-.git
cd Parasoft-License-Monitor-by-ESL-

# Run locally (same machine as DTP)
python monitor.py --logs-path "C:\ProgramData\Parasoft\DTP\logs"

# Or run remotely (agent on DTP server)
python agent.py --logs-path "..." --token <token>
python monitor.py --remote http://dtp-server:9271 --token <token>

# Output: interactive HTML dashboard + CSV files
Get Started on GitHub
